questions.question: As a type of access control, which of the following asks for identifying occurrences?
questions.explanation: Explanation: There are different types of access control. Access controls can be categorized as follows: Preventive (in order to avoid occurrence) Detective (in order to detect or identify occurrences) Deterrent (in order to discourage occurrences) Corrective (In order to correct or restore controls) Recovery (in order to restore resources, capabilities, or losses)

questions.question: As a type of access control, which of the following asks for discouraging occurrence?
questions.explanation: Explanation: There are different types of access control. Access controls can be categorized as follows: Preventive (in order to avoid occurrence) Detective (in order to detect or identify occurrences) Deterrent (in order to discourage occurrences) Corrective (In order to correct or restore controls) Recovery (in order to restore resources, capabilities, or losses)

questions.explanation: Explanation: There are different types of access control. Access controls can be categorized as follows: Preventive (in order to avoid occurrence) Detective (in order to detect or identify occurrences) Deterrent (in order to discourage occurrences) Corrective (In order to correct or restore controls) Recovery (in order to restore resources, capabilities, or losses)

questions.question: Access control is the collection of mechanisms that permits managers of a system to exercise influence over the use of:
questions.explanation: Explanation: Access control is the collection of mechanisms that permits managers of a system to exercise a directing or restraining influence over the behavior, use, and content of a system. It permits management to specify what users can do, which resources they can access, and what operations they can perform on a system.

questions.question: What fencing height is likely to stop a determined intruder?

options.C: ) 8' high and above with strands of barbed wire
options.D: ) No fence can stop a determined intruder

Which issue when selecting a facility site deals with the surrounding terrain, building markings and signs, and high or low population in the area?

The main risks that physical security components combat are all of the following EXCEPT:

Examples of types of physical access controls include all except which of the following?
Which of the following is the most costly countermeasures to reducing physical security risks?
Which of the following protection devices is used for spot protection within a few inches of the object, rather than for overall room security monitoring?
Which of the following questions is less likely to help in assessing physical access controls?

options.A: ) Does management regularly review the list of persons with physical access to sensitive facilities?
options.B: ) Is the operating system configured to prevent circumvention of the security software and application controls?
options.C: ) Are keys or other access devices needed to enter the computer room and media library?
options.D: ) Are visitors to sensitive areas signed in and escorted?

questions.question: The concentric circle approach is used to
questions.explanation: The original answer for this question was C (assess the communications network security) however I think the concentric circle is defining what in the krutz book is know as the security perimeter. To this end this is a reference "A circular security perimeter that is under the access control defines the area or zone to be protected. Preventive/physical controls include fences, badges, multiple doors (man-traps that consists of two doors physically separated so that an individual can be 'trapped' in the space between the doors after entering one of the doors), magnetic card entry systems, biometrics (for identification), guards, dogs, environmental control systems (temperature, humidity, and so forth), and building and access area layout." -Ronald Krutz The CISSP PREP Guide (gold edition) pg 13 This is a standard concentric circle model shown in Figure 1 . If you've never seen this, you haven't had a security lecture. On the outside is our perimeter. We are fortunate to have some defenses on our base. Although some bases don't have people guarding the gates and checking IDs any longer, there's still the perception that it's tougher to commit a crime on a Naval base than it would be at GM. The point is: How much control do we have over fencing and guards? The answer: Not much. The next circle, the red circle, contains your internal access controls. For our purposes, the heart of the red circle is the computer. That's what I want to zero in on. The internal controls are the things you can do to keep people out of your PCs and off your network. http://www.chips.navy.mil/archives/96_oct/file5.htm

questions.question: The MAIN reason for developing closed-circuit television (CCTV) as part of your physical security program is to

options.A: Provide hard evidence for criminal prosecution.

questions.explanation: A CCTV enables a guard to monitor many different areas at once from a centralized location.- Shon Harris All-in-one CISSP Certification Guide pg 179-180

questions.question: Closed circuit TV is a feature of:
questions.explanation: Explanation: Detective Physical Controls would use the following: motion detectors, closed circuit TV, sensors, and alarms.

questions.question: Motion detector is a feature of:
questions.explanation: Explanation: Detective Physical Controls would use the following: motion detectors, closed circuit TV, sensors, and alarms.

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical control?
Which of the following is a detective control?
The basic Electronic Access Control (EAC) components required for access doors are an electromagnetic lock,

options.A: A credential reader, and a door closed sensor.
options.B: A card reader, and a door open sensor.
options.C: A biometric reader, and a door open sensor.
options.D: A card reader, and door motion detector.

questions.explanation: We have not been able to find any reference to this question really. So we are going with A "In addition to smart and dumb cards, proximity readers can be used to control physical access. A proximity reader can be passive device, a field-powered device, or a transponder." - Ed Tittle CISSP Study Guide (sybex) pg 650

questions.question: Which of the following control pairing places emphasis on "soft" mechanisms that support the access control objectives?
questions.explanation: "Preventive-Administrative The following are the soft mechanisms that are put into place to enforce access control and protection for the company as a whole: Policies and procedures Effective hiring practices Pre-employment background checks Controlled termination processes Data classification and labeling Security awareness" Pg. 157 Shon Harris: All-In-One CISSP Certification Guide.

questions.question: Controls like guards and general steps to maintain building security, securing of server rooms or laptops, the protection of cables, and the backing up of files are some of the examples of:
Which of the following is NOT a type of motion detector?
questions.explanation: Explanation: Audio detector detects sound not motion Not A: A photoelectric sensor is a motion sensor that's what it was designed to do.

questions.question: Which of the following measures would be the BEST deterrent to the theft of corporate information from a laptop which was left in a hotel room?

options.A: ) Store all data on disks and lock them in an in-room safe
options.B: ) Remove the batteries and power supply from the laptop and store them separately from the computer
options.C: ) Install a cable lock on the laptop when it is unattended
options.D: ) Encrypt the data on the hard drive

options.C: ) The operation of access control devices
options.D: ) The need to detect unauthorized access

options.D: ) No fence can stop a determined intruder

questions.question: A controlled light fixture mounted on a 5-meter pole can illuminate an area 30 meter in diameter. For security lighting purposes, what would be the proper distance between fixtures?
questions.explanation: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) standard pertaining to perimeter protection states that critical areas should be illuminated eight feet high and two feet out. (It is referred to as two-feet candles that reach eight feet in height) - Shon Harris All-in-one CISSP Certification Guide pg 325

questions.question: Critical areas should be lighted:

options.A: ) Eight feet high and two feet out
options.B: ) Eight feet high and four feet out
options.C: ) Ten feet high and four feet out
options.D: ) Ten feet high and six feet out

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding an off-site information processing facility is TRUE?

options.A: ) It should have the same amount of physical access restrictions as the primary processing unit
options.B: ) It should be located in proximity to the originating site so that it can quicl be made operational
options.C: ) It should be easily identified from the outside so in the event of an emergency it can be easily found
options.D: ) Need not have the same level of environmental monitoring as the originating site since this would be cost prohibitive
